title: Quadratic Equations
summary: Chapter 4 introduces quadratic equations of the form ax² + bx + c = 0. Students learn to check whether a given equation is quadratic and find its roots using three methods: factorisation, completing the square, and the quadratic formula. The nature of roots is determined using the discriminant D = b² - 4ac — whether roots are real and distinct, real and equal, or non-real. The chapter includes practical word problems modelled as quadratic equations.
learningPoints: Identify a quadratic equation of the form ax² + bx + c = 0 where a is not equal to 0
Verify whether a given value is a root by substitution
Solve quadratic equations by factorisation method
Solve quadratic equations by completing the square method
Derive and apply the quadratic formula x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac)) / 2a
Calculate discriminant D = b² - 4ac to determine nature of roots
D > 0 means two distinct real roots, D = 0 means two equal real roots, D < 0 means no real roots
Form a quadratic equation when roots are given
Solve real-life word problems using quadratic equations
Recognise equations reducible to quadratic form

formulas.label: Quadratic Formula
formulas.description: Sridharacharya formula to find roots directly.

formulas.label: Discriminant
formulas.description: D > 0: distinct real roots. D = 0: equal roots. D < 0: no real roots.

formulas.label: Sum of Roots
formulas.description: Sum of both roots of the quadratic.

formulas.label: Product of Roots
formulas.description: Product of both roots of the quadratic.

formulas.label: Form Quadratic from Roots
formulas.description: Build quadratic when roots are known.

resources.label: Quadratic Equations — Video Lessons
resources.description: Videos on factorisation, completing the square and quadratic formula.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a quadratic equation?
mcqs.explanation: A quadratic equation has degree 2. Only 2x² - 3x + 1 = 0 fits the form ax² + bx + c = 0.

mcqs.question: If D = 0 for a quadratic equation, the roots are:
mcqs.explanation: D = 0 gives x = -b/2a twice — two equal real roots.

mcqs.question: Roots of x² + x - 2 = 0 using quadratic formula are:
For what value of k does kx² + 2x + 1 = 0 have equal roots?
mcqs.explanation: For equal roots: D = 4 - 4k = 0, so k = 1.

mcqs.question: Product of two consecutive positive integers is 306. The integers are:
The sum of roots of 2x² - 5x + 3 = 0 is:
If one root of x² - 3x + k = 0 is 1, then k equals:
A train travels 360 km. If speed were 5 km/h more it takes 1 hour less. Speed is:
For ax² + bx + c = 0, roots are real and distinct when:
mcqs.explanation: D > 0 gives two distinct real roots.

mcqs.question: Which method always works to solve any quadratic equation?
options: D. Both completing the square and quadratic formula
mcqs.explanation: Both completing the square and quadratic formula work for all quadratic equations.

exercises.question: Find discriminant and nature of roots of 2x² - 6x + 3 = 0.
exercises.answer: D = 36 - 24 = 12 > 0. Roots are real and distinct.

exercises.question: Find k so that (k-12)x² + 2(k-12)x + 2 = 0 has equal roots.
Sum of areas of two squares is 468 m². Difference of perimeters is 24 m. Find sides.
An express train takes 1 hour less than passenger train for 132 km and is 11 km/h faster. Find speeds.
Two taps fill a tank in 9.375 hours. Larger tap takes 10 hours less. Find individual times.
Diagonal of rectangular field is 60m more than shorter side. Longer side is 30m more than shorter. Find sides.
